question_text: A permanent magnet is cut in half along its length (parallel to the poles). What is the result?

options.label: A
options.text: You create one north pole and one south pole

options.label: B
options.text: You destroy the magnetic properties completely

options.label: C
options.text: You create two new magnets, each with their own north and south poles

options.label: D
options.text: The two halves repel each other permanently

explanation: Magnetic poles cannot be isolated—every magnet has both a north and south pole. When you cut a magnet, you create two smaller magnets, each with their own pair of poles. This is because magnetism comes from the alignment of atomic particles throughout the material.
